architecture_overview:
SchoolAutomaton is a Dockerized two-service app (FastAPI backend + Vue 3 SPA) sharing one SQLite database. The contract that lets multiple engineers build in parallel is a strict one-way data flow and a small set of frozen interfaces.

CORE FLOW (student): raw free-text prompt -> [Module A: Sanitization pipeline, 6 layers] produces a frozen Pydantic StructuredIntent -> Decision router (Proceed / Clarify / Refuse / Crisis) -> [Module B: Generation orchestrator, Opus 4.8] builds prompts ONLY from validated StructuredIntent fields (never the raw text) -> either a LESSON (12-section skeleton, dual-coded, FSRS-card-bearing items) or a TEST (typed questions) -> persisted to SQLite + content-hashed assets on disk -> [Module C: Frontend] renders LessonReader or TestRunner -> answers POSTed -> [Module D: Gamification + FSRS] updates XP/streak/badges/mastery and FSRS schedule -> Results.

THE FIVE PARALLELIZABLE MODULES + THEIR FROZEN SEAMS:
- A. Sanitization & safety (backend/app/sanitization/*): owns StructuredIntent and the Decision union. Everyone downstream consumes StructuredIntent; nobody re-reads raw_prompt.
- B. LLM generation (backend/app/llm/*): owns the Pydantic content schemas (LessonPlan, LessonSection, Item, Quiz, QuizQuestion, VisualSpec) emitted via Claude Opus 4.8 structured outputs. Persists rows + emits SSE progress.
- C. Pedagogy/scheduling/gamification services (backend/app/services/srs_service.py, leveling.py, gamification.py, mastery.py): pure-ish functions keyed off structured DB fields (FSRS card json, bloom_tier, concept_id, mastery).
- D. Visual pipeline (backend/app/visuals/*): hybrid Claude-SVG (default) + Replicate FLUX.2 (raster) + content-hash disk cache. Owns visual_assets table + /assets/{hash} route.
- E. Frontend SPA (frontend/src/*): Vue 3.5 + Pinia 3, three flows, hard-sanitizes all LLM/Replicate output.

INTEGRATION-DRIFT GUARDS: (1) a single shared OpenAPI 3.1 schema generated by FastAPI is the source of truth for TS types (openapi-typescript -> src/types/api.ts). (2) Pydantic v2 models for StructuredIntent, Lesson/Section/Item, Quiz/Question, and all API responses live in backend/app/schemas/ and are imported by both the sanitizer and generator. (3) The FSRS card is ALWAYS round-tripped via py-fsrs Card.to_json()/from_json() stored in a Text column; no module computes FSRS math by hand. (4) PROMPT_VERSION and MODEL_ID are central config constants stamped on every generated row for auditability and cache-busting. (5) The resume code is a server-issued opaque token; the server is the only source of truth for authorization (admin auth is fully separate JWT). Async stack throughout: SQLAlchemy 2.0 async + aiosqlite, AsyncAnthropic, async replicate client, FastAPI background tasks for visual generation. Alembic manages all migrations.

columns: resume_code TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL (8-char Crockford base32, server-issued, e.g. 'K7QF-2M9X')
resume_code_hash TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL (sha256 of resume_code; lookups use the hash, never the raw)
display_name TEXT NULL (optional self-chosen nickname, sanitized)
primary_language TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'en' (BCP-47, last detected)
desired_retention REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.90 (FSRS knob: 0.85 casual / 0.90 default / 0.95 deadline)
db_schema.notes: Anonymous profile. resume_code is the only credential a learner has; show it once on creation and on /resume. Index on resume_code_hash. No PII required or solicited (COPPA 2026).

columns: interleave_strength REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.3 (0..1; lowered for youngest band)
rest_days_per_week INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (1 default for under-13/early_primary band)
db_schema.notes: 1:1 with profiles. Mirrors the client usePrefs store so prefs survive resume on a new device. Server is source of truth; client localStorage is a cache.

columns: request_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL (uuid4, returned to client as sessionId)
profile_id INTEGER FK->profiles.id NULL (null until profile attached)
structured_intent_json TEXT NOT NULL (the validated StructuredIntent Pydantic dump)
db_schema.notes: One row per student prompt. Raw prompt text is NEVER stored here (only in sanitization_audit, and there only hashed unless RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG). status drives the SSE /loading screen.

columns: request_id TEXT NOT NULL (FK-ish to learning_requests.request_id)
ip_hash TEXT NOT NULL (salted sha256 of client IP/device)
removed_char_summary TEXT NULL (json: counts of stripped invisible/bidi/control chars)
heuristic_hit_ids TEXT NULL (json array of patterns.yaml rule ids)
classifier_verdict_json TEXT NOT NULL (StructuredIntent with topic HASHED)
raw_prompt_encrypted TEXT NULL (ONLY populated when RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG=true AND a safety_flag fired; N-day purge)
db_schema.notes: Default stores NO raw text. RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG (env, default false) lets admins retain raw text for flagged events only, auto-purged after settings.audit_raw_retention_days.
Shared concept catalog. Powers prereq activation, interleaving, knowledge map, unlock gating. Grown lazily by the generator (INSERT OR IGNORE on slug).
Directed graph edges. prerequisite -> ZPD gating + prior-knowledge activation; related -> interleaving neighborhood. UNIQUE(from,to,edge_type).

columns: refutation_text TEXT NOT NULL (multilingual NOT required here; generated refutation lives on the item)
db_schema.notes: Catalog of known misconceptions per concept. Each quiz distractor references a misconception_id so wrong answers route to targeted refutation.

columns: measured_fkgl REAL NULL (textstat result; NULL for non-English with proxy note)
objectives_json TEXT NOT NULL (array of {text, bloom_tier, concept_id})
content_cache_key TEXT NULL (sha256 of normalized intent for library reuse)
db_schema.notes: One lesson per study request. content_cache_key enables cross-student reuse of 'photosynthesis grade 5' style lessons (Batches pre-gen). Index content_cache_key.
Association of a lesson to concepts. UNIQUE(lesson_id,concept_id,relation).

columns: body_markdown TEXT NULL (sanitized client-side before render)
gated BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (true => locked until prior interactive section attempted)
db_schema.notes: Ordered sections implementing the fixed lesson skeleton. Frontend renders by ordinal; explanation/worked_example carry asset refs; pretest/practice/misconception_check reference items.

columns: label_overlay_json TEXT NULL (translated SVG <text> overlay positions for language-neutral raster)
db_schema.notes: Join between content and the content-addressed visual cache. label_overlay_json lets one cached raster serve all languages via vector text overlay.

columns: item_difficulty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 3 (1..5 adaptive, mutated at runtime)
payload_json TEXT NOT NULL (kind-specific: options[], pairs[], regions[], blanks[])
hint_ladder_json TEXT NULL (array of progressive hints)
db_schema.notes: The reviewable unit. Shared by lessons (pretest/practice/misconception_check) and quizzes (via quiz_questions). correct answers + explanation are SERVER-ONLY (never sent to client at delivery; revealed by grading endpoint). Index concept_id.

columns: fsrs_card_json TEXT NOT NULL (py-fsrs Card.to_json() round-trip; SOURCE OF TRUTH)
db_schema.notes: Per (profile,item) FSRS card. The denormalized columns (state/stability/difficulty/due) are read-only projections for indexing; fsrs_card_json is authoritative. UNIQUE(profile_id,item_id). Index (profile_id, due) for the due-items scheduler query.
A test instance. Boss_review composes from FSRS-due items rather than freshly generated ones.
Ordered join from quiz to the shared items pool. Lets the same item appear in lessons and quizzes and carry one FSRS card.
One attempt per quiz run. accuracy feeds adaptive-difficulty rolling window.

columns: attempt_id INTEGER FK->quiz_attempts.id NULL (null for lesson-embedded practice)
db_schema.notes: Immutable answer log. fsrs_rating is computed deterministically server-side (see security/pedagogy) then fed to srs_service. detected_misconception_id comes from the matched distractor.

columns: mastery REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0 (0..1, DERIVED from FSRS retrievability+state across the concept's items)
decay_due_at TIMESTAMP NULL (min due of the concept's mastered items)
db_schema.notes: One row per (profile,concept). mastery is NOT a separate SM-2 model: mastery = mean over the concept's items of FSRS retrievability R(t,S) weighted by state, clamped 0..1. mastered when mastery>=0.85; needs_review when now>decay_due_at. UNIQUE(profile_id,concept_id).
Event-sourced XP ledger. profiles.total_xp is a denormalized SUM; level derived on read via leveling.py. Never decrement XP.
1:1 with profiles. settle_streak() job applies freezes/rest-days silently before breaking; 48h earn-back window. Freeze cap 2 (5 for >=30-day streaks). Never purchasable.

columns: criterion_key TEXT NOT NULL (evaluator function key)
db_schema.notes: Seeded as data. ~15 mastery-anchored badges. criterion_key maps to a Python evaluator run after each learning event against xp_events + skill_mastery.
Locked badges still get a row to show progress (e.g. 3/5). UNIQUE(profile_id,badge_id). unlocked_at NULL while in progress.

columns: hash TEXT PK (sha256 of canonical JSON {asset_type,model_id,model_version,normalized_prompt,sorted_params,seed,output_format})
prompt TEXT NOT NULL (rewritten, kid-safe; never raw student text)
lang TEXT NULL (NULL for language-neutral raster)
svg_inline TEXT NULL (sanitized SVG stored inline for asset_type=svg)
db_schema.notes: Content-addressed cache. INSERT OR IGNORE on hash (SQLite single-writer). LRU eviction by last_accessed_at + low hit_count. Served via /assets/{hash} with Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable.
Separate auth realm from learner resume codes. JWT (HS256) login. Argon2id hashing (argon2-cffi).

columns: is_secret BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (true => value encrypted at rest with APP_SECRET via Fernet)
db_schema.notes: KV store for runtime config + API keys (anthropic_api_key, replicate_api_token), mechanic kill-switches per age band, leaderboard_enabled, audit_raw_retention_days, XP curve constants. Secrets Fernet-encrypted; never returned in plaintext via API (masked). Env vars override DB for keys at boot.
Cost + cache-hit telemetry. cache_read_tokens>0 verifies prompt caching is working. Aggregated for admin dashboards.

api_endpoints.purpose: Create a new anonymous profile and issue a resume code.
api_endpoints.response: 201 { resume_code: 'K7QF-2M9X', profile: { id, total_xp:0, level:1, age_band, primary_language }, settings: {...} } -- resume_code shown ONCE; client caches in localStorage.

api_endpoints.purpose: Resume an existing profile from a resume code.
api_endpoints.response: 200 ProfileEnvelope { profile, settings, gamification:{level,total_xp,xp_to_next,streak,badges_count} } | 404 if unknown.

api_endpoints.purpose: Fetch current profile by resume code header (X-Resume-Code).
api_endpoints.response: 200 ProfileEnvelope | 401.

api_endpoints.purpose: Update accessibility/learning prefs (theme, font, font_scale, reduced_motion, sound, locale, daily_goal, desired_retention).
api_endpoints.response: 200 ProfileSettings.

api_endpoints.purpose: Submit a raw student prompt; runs the 6-layer sanitize pipeline and returns a routing Decision. THE single entrypoint for untrusted text.
api_endpoints.request: { prompt: string (<=2000 chars), resume_code?: string } + header X-Resume-Code; rate-limited per (profile,ip_hash) 20/min,300/day.
api_endpoints.response: 200 Decision union: {type:'proceed', request_id, mode:'study'|'test', intent:StructuredIntent} | {type:'clarify', request_id, question, suggestions[]} | {type:'refuse', reason, redirect_suggestions[]} | {type:'crisis', resources:[{name,phone,url,country}], disclosure} . 429 on rate limit. Raw prompt NEVER echoed.

api_endpoints.purpose: Kick off async generation (lesson or quiz) for a proceed-decision request. Returns immediately; work runs in a background task.
api_endpoints.response: 202 { request_id, status:'queued' }.

api_endpoints.purpose: SSE stream of generation progress for the /loading screen.
api_endpoints.response: text/event-stream events: {event:'status', data:{status:'generating'}} ; {event:'plan', data:{sections:[stub]}} ; {event:'section', data:{ordinal,kind,title}} ; {event:'ready', data:{mode,lesson_id|quiz_id}} ; {event:'error', data:{message}}.

api_endpoints.purpose: Poll fallback for generation status when SSE unavailable.
api_endpoints.response: 200 { request_id, status, mode, lesson_id?, quiz_id? }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Fetch a fully generated lesson for the LessonReader.
api_endpoints.response: 200 Lesson { id, topic, language, grade_band, objectives[], measured_fkgl, lexile_band, sections:[{ordinal,kind,title,body_markdown,gated,assets:[{hash,layout_slot,alt_text,caption,url:'/assets/{hash}'}],items:[ItemPublic]}] } . ItemPublic OMITS correct answers/explanation.

api_endpoints.purpose: Fetch a quiz for the TestRunner (delivery view, answers withheld).
api_endpoints.response: 200 Quiz { id, title, language, quiz_type, questions:[{question_id, item_id, ordinal, points, kind, stem_markdown, payload(options/pairs/regions WITHOUT correctness), hint_available:bool}] } . correct_index/expected_answer/explanation NEVER sent here.

api_endpoints.purpose: Start a quiz attempt.
api_endpoints.response: 201 { attempt_id, started_at }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Grade a single answer (deterministic + optional LLM grading for short_answer/explain), update FSRS, mastery, XP, badges, streak. The unified grading endpoint.
api_endpoints.request: { item_id, attempt_id?, submitted_value, used_hint:bool, latency_ms } + X-Resume-Code
api_endpoints.response: 200 GradeResult { is_correct, partial_credit, fsrs_rating, next_due, explanation, misconception?:{description,refutation}, feedback:{text(localized, process/strategy framed), encouragement_focus}, xp_awarded, combo_multiplier, mastery_delta, new_badges:[{code,title}], level_up?:{from,to} }. correct answer revealed HERE, not at delivery.

api_endpoints.purpose: Finalize an attempt, compute score/accuracy, run end-of-session badge eval + streak settle.
api_endpoints.response: 200 ResultsSummary { score, max_score, accuracy, xp_awarded, combo_max, new_badges[], streak:{current,longest,frozen?:bool}, mastery_changes:[{concept,name,before,after,state}] }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Get the FSRS-due review session (interleaved), capped per daily policy.
api_endpoints.request: X-Resume-Code; query ?limit (default min(daily_cap)) ; daily_cap = 20 new + all overdue (10 new for early_primary).
api_endpoints.response: 200 { items:[ItemPublic], composition:{current:0.55,related:0.25,prereq:0.20} } -- shuffled so consecutive items differ in concept/type.

api_endpoints.purpose: Submit a review rating directly (review flow, no quiz attempt). Wraps srs_service.review_card.
api_endpoints.request: { rating:1|2|3|4 } | { submitted_value, used_hint, latency_ms } (server derives rating) + X-Resume-Code
api_endpoints.response: 200 { next_due, mastery_delta, xp_awarded, new_badges[] }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Knowledge map: concept nodes with mastery/state for the self-referenced progress visualization.
api_endpoints.response: 200 { nodes:[{concept_id, title, subject, mastery, state, prereq_ids[], related_ids[], last_reviewed, decay_due_at}], edges:[{from,to,type}] }.

api_endpoints.purpose: XP/level/streak/badges/daily-goal snapshot for header + results.
api_endpoints.response: 200 { level, total_xp, xp_to_next, level_progress_pct, streak:{current,longest,freeze_inventory,is_perfect}, daily_goal, daily_progress_xp, badges:[{code,title,tier,unlocked_at,progress}] }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Serve a cached visual asset (svg/raster/icon/video) by content hash.
api_endpoints.response: 200 image/webp|image/svg+xml|video/mp4 with Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable. 404 if evicted.

api_endpoints.purpose: Lazy-loaded UI string bundle for vue-i18n.
api_endpoints.response: 200 { ...messages } with ETag.

api_endpoints.purpose: Admin login (separate realm).
api_endpoints.response: 200 { access_token (JWT, 1h), token_type:'bearer' } | 401.

api_endpoints.purpose: Admin overview: usage, cost, cache-hit rate, safety incidents.
api_endpoints.response: 200 { requests_24h, decisions_breakdown, anthropic_cost_usd, replicate_cost_usd, cache_hit_rate, crisis_events, refusals }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Browse sanitization audit log (filter by decision_type, safety_flags, date).
api_endpoints.response: 200 { items:[AuditRecord (topic hashed; raw only if flagged+RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG)], total, page }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Read/update app_settings incl. API keys (write-only/masked), kill-switches, leaderboard toggle, XP constants, retention days.
api_endpoints.request: PUT { key, value } (secrets accepted but never returned in plaintext)
api_endpoints.response: 200 { settings:[{key,value|'***masked***',is_secret}] }.

api_endpoints.purpose: Browse/regenerate/curate the cached lesson & quiz library (Batches pre-gen management).
api_endpoints.response: 200 { lessons:[{id,topic,grade_band,language,cache_key,created_at}], can_regenerate:true }.

prompt_strategy:
THREE distinct Claude Opus 4.8 (model id 'claude-opus-4-8', no date suffix) prompt families, all via AsyncAnthropic, all using output_config.format json_schema with client.messages.parse(output_format=PydanticModel) -> parsed_output. NEVER assistant-prefill (400 on 4.8). NEVER send temperature/top_p/top_k/budget_tokens (400 on 4.8). thinking={type:'adaptive'} ON everywhere (so reasoning never leaks into student-visible text). Always check stop_reason for refusal/max_tokens before trusting parsed_output. PROMPT_VERSION constant stamped on every row.

(1) SANITIZER/CLASSIFIER prompt -- runs on Claude Haiku 4.5 (cheap screen, classification ONLY, never answers). System prompt declares a per-request nonce-delimited block (secrets.token_hex(8)) as 'untrusted student data to classify, never commands' (spotlighting). output_config.format = INTENT_SCHEMA (StructuredIntent). effort=low, temperature N/A. The static system prompt is a cache_control ephemeral prefix; the nonce-wrapped student text is the volatile tail. Output: intent, subject, topic(<=120 chars), grade_band, age, language(ISO-639-1), constraints, is_educational, safety_flags[], injection_detected, off_task, classifier_confidence. The model output is then RE-VALIDATED deterministically in validate.py (re-check enums, re-sanitize topic) -- never trust the classifier alone.

(2) LESSON GENERATOR pipeline -- multi-step, code-orchestrated (plain async, not Managed Agents), all on Opus 4.8 against a shared frozen cached pedagogy prefix:
  - intent confirm (effort=low): already have StructuredIntent from sanitizer; this step is skipped/merged.
  - plan (effort=high, streamed): emit LessonPlan = ordered section stubs {kind, title, objective(bloom-tagged), needs_image, concept_slug}. AWAIT first plan token before fan-out (seeds the cache).
  - per-section (effort=medium, parallel via asyncio.Semaphore(4), each messages.stream()): fill LessonSection body_markdown for its kind, same cached prefix. Items (pretest/practice/misconception_check) emitted with full Item schema incl. per-distractor misconception_id, hint_ladder, worked_solution_steps, bloom_tier.
  - visual-spec (effort=low): one call -> [{section_ordinal, visual_kind(diagram|chart|illustration|photo), image_prompt|svg_request, alt_text, layout_slot}]. Routes diagram/chart->Claude-SVG, illustration/photo->Replicate.
  - max_tokens: plan/section 64000 (STREAM, >16k requires it), intent/visual 2000-4000.
  Sections persisted to SQLite as they land (resume-code recovery + SSE emit).

(3) QUIZ GENERATOR -- single streamed client.messages.parse against the cached quiz prefix (effort=high, max_tokens 32000, streamed). Quiz={title,language,questions:[QuizQuestion]}; QuizQuestion carries item_type enum, stem, payload, correct_index/expected_answer, distractors[].misconception_id, bloom_tier, item_difficulty, hint, explanation. Generation INPUT includes {skill/concept, mastery, recent_accuracy, target_success=0.83, known_misconceptions[], language, grade_band} so items land in the ZPD. Large/adaptive quizzes add a low-effort blueprint step first.

(4) ANSWER GRADER (for short_answer/numeric/explain_it only; mcq/true_false/cloze/match/order/hotspot graded deterministically) -- Opus 4.8 structured output {correct:bool, partial_credit:0..1, concept_tags[], misconception:str|null, explanation:str, encouragement_focus:'effort'|'strategy'|'progress'}.

PROMPT CACHING (cost control across the many per-student calls): Each content family has a SYSTEM_PEDAGOGY constant (the big evidence-based pedagogy + lesson-skeleton + Bloom + readability + feedback-rules block, PER LANGUAGE for native exemplars) as a cache_control:{type:'ephemeral'} prefix that MUST be byte-identical (NO datetime.now, NO resume code/uuid, NO unsorted json.dumps). Min cacheable prefix on 4.8 = 4096 tokens (pad to clear it). Volatile data (date, profile id, language, mastery, concrete topic) goes in the trailing user message ONLY. Order tools->system->messages. Pre-warm at startup with max_tokens=0. Verify usage.cache_read_input_tokens>0; log to generation_usage. 5m TTL default, 1h only for the curated-library Batches job. SDK max_retries=5 (auto-retry 429/5xx/529 only); count_tokens() before expensive fan-out. Use anthropic[aiohttp] + DefaultAioHttpClient for concurrency. Bulk library pre-gen uses the Batches API (50% off).

Schema-constraint nuance: minimum/maximum/minLength/maxLength/recursive are STRIPPED from json_schema by Opus 4.8 -- enforce 'exactly 4 options', 'difficulty 1-5', counts/bounds in the prompt text AND in Pydantic field validators, regenerate (max 2 retries) on validation failure. additionalProperties:false on every object. enum/const/anyOf/allOf/$ref are supported. Multilingual: language threaded into the volatile tail, all student-facing values localized, all JSON keys English.
pedagogy_framework:
SPACED REPETITION (single source of truth): FSRS-6 via py-fsrs ('fsrs>=6.3.1', MIT). NO hand-coded SM-2 / weight math anywhere -- treat py-fsrs internals as a black box. srs_service.py wraps one Scheduler(desired_retention=<profile knob 0.85/0.90/0.95>, learning_steps=(1min,10min), relearning_steps=(10min,), maximum_interval=36500, enable_fuzzing=True). Each reviewable Item has a per-profile FSRS Card persisted via Card.to_json() in item_fsrs_cards.fsrs_card_json (authoritative) with denormalized state/stability/difficulty/due columns for indexing. On answer: card, log = scheduler.review_card(card, rating); persist; next due = card.due (UTC). due_items query orders by due asc, caps daily new at 20 (10 for early_primary) + all overdue.

RATING DERIVATION (deterministic, server-side, the bridge between grading and FSRS): Again(1)=wrong/incorrect; Hard(2)=correct but used_hint OR latency_ms above the item's slow threshold; Good(3)=correct, no hint, normal speed; Easy(4)=correct, no hint, fast (latency below quick threshold). This single mapping is used by both /answers and /review/{item_id}.

MASTERY (derived, NOT a second algorithm): skill_mastery.mastery = clamp(mean over the concept's items of FSRS retrievability R(t,S) at now, weighted up for state=Review and down for New/Learning, 0..1). node_state: locked (prereqs unmet) -> available -> learning -> mastered (mastery>=0.85) -> needs_review (now>decay_due_at = min due of the concept's mastered items). This unifies dossier-1 FSRS and dossier-2 'per-skill mastery score' into one model and powers the knowledge tree + badges.

LESSON SKELETON (fixed ordered enum, every lesson): hook, objectives (3-5 Bloom-tagged 'I can...' statements), prior_knowledge (pull prereq concepts), pretest (ungraded, EXPLICITLY framed as low-stakes), explanation (concrete->abstract, dual-coded: every key concept + a visual + one-line caption), worked_example, faded_example (progressive blanks), practice, interleaved_review (mix current + related-concept due + prereq refreshers), elaboration (self-explanation/elaborative-interrogation prompts), misconception_check (targets a tagged misconception with corrective feedback), summary (+ spaced-review preview + growth-mindset close). Later interactive sections gate until earlier ones attempted.

READABILITY (deterministic verification): map grade_band -> target FKGL + Lexile band [K:FKGL<=1,BR40L-230L; G1-2:1-2,190-650L; G3-5:3-5,520-1010L; G6-8:6-8,925-1185L; G9-12:9-12,1050-1385L; adult:9-11,~1100-1300L] + sentence/vocab caps [K-G2 <=10 words/sentence,<=3 new terms; G3-5 <=15,~5 define inline; G6-8 glossary; G9-12 full register]. After generation, textstat.flesch_kincaid_grade() per explanation section; if measured > target+1.5, re-prompt to simplify (max 2x); store target_fkgl+measured_fkgl+lexile_band. Non-English: FKGL unreliable -> use proxy (avg sentence length + long-word share), store readability_note, do not store a misleading FKGL.

BLOOM DIFFICULTY SPINE (1-6: Remember,Understand,Apply,Analyze,Evaluate,Create): each objective gets a Bloom verb; each item gets bloom_tier + difficulty. Grade weighting: K-G5 emphasize tiers 1-3; G6-12+adult include 4-6. Adaptive stepper (in quiz service, NOT the LLM): start at learner's current mastery tier; +1 tier after 2 consecutive correct; -1 after a miss. Target ~0.83 success rate (range 0.80-0.85) using rolling recent_accuracy over last ~10 items, fed into the generation prompt.

INTERLEAVING: review/practice session mix ~50-60% current concept, 20-30% related-concept due items, 10-20% prerequisite refreshers, shuffled so consecutive items differ in concept/type; neighborhood bounded by concept_edges(related); interleave_strength reduced for early_primary.

FEEDBACK (growth-mindset, multilingual, injected into prompts): praise strategy/process never innate ability; on error use 'not yet' + next actionable step + optional hint, never bare 'wrong'; never praise effort alone without a strategy/correction; self-referenced framing ('you've now got 4/5 — last week 1/5'), never norm-referenced. 2-3 templated exemplars per outcome in the prompt, generated in detected language.
gamification_design:
PRINCIPLE (resolves dossier-2 vs naive points): every reward wraps a genuine learning signal; XP is a side-effect of mastery, never of activity/time/clicks. Mechanics are age-band-aware with per-mechanic admin kill-switches.

XP CURVE (leveling.py, pure functions, constants config-driven via app_settings): BASE_XP=100, CURVE_EXP=1.5. xp_total(L)=round(100*L**1.5); level_from_xp(t)=floor((t/100)**(1/1.5)); xp_to_next(t)=xp_total(level+1)-t. (L2=283, L3=520, L5=1118, L10=3162, L20=8944 cumulative.) Store only profiles.total_xp (SUM of xp_events); derive level on read to avoid drift.

XP PER ANSWER (diminishing returns prevent grinding): xp = round(BASE_ITEM_XP * difficulty_mult * (1 - mastery_before)), BASE_ITEM_XP=10, difficulty_mult = item_difficulty(1..5)/3. Re-grinding a mastered node (mastery~1) yields ~0 XP. XP reasons: first_try_correct, spaced_retention (correct after a delay), mastery_gain, comeback (fixed a previously-failed node), combo_bonus, daily_goal, badge.

COMBO: session multiplier for consecutive first-try-correct, capped 2.0x at 8-combo; wrong answer only resets to 1.0x (never a penalty/loss).

STREAKS (streak_state, settle_streak() job): bounded, mostly-invisible forgiveness. Freeze inventory earned through play only (never purchasable), cap 2 (up to 5 at >=30-day streak), auto-applied silently (shows 'frozen' icon, not a break). 48h earn-back/repair window. Configurable rest_days_per_week (default 1 for early_primary/under-13, 0 otherwise) that don't break the streak. is_perfect flag = no freezes used = prestige halo. Reminders are gentle/gain-framed, NEVER loss-framed FOMO.

~15 MASTERY-ANCHORED BADGES (badge_definitions seed, criterion_key evaluators run after each learning event): first_light(master 1st node), root_system(master all prereqs of a topic), deep_diver(mastery>=0.9 on a node), comeback(failed->mastered), spaced_master(correct after 7-day gap), sharpshooter(10 first-try-correct in a session), honest_explorer(used hint/'not sure' then correct -- rewards metacognition not guessing), polyglot(learn in 2+ languages), cross_pollinator(connect concepts across 2 subject trees), curiosity(5 follow-up/why questions), self_beater(beat own previous accuracy on a retest), tree_grower(25/50/100% of a tree branch -- TIERED), teacher(LLM-graded correct teach-back via explain_it quiz), resilient(return+complete after a streak break), marathon_of_mastery(master 50 nodes). NO 'logged in 30 days' / 'answered 1000 questions' activity counters. Locked badges show progress (3/5) via profile_badges.progress_numerator/denominator.

KNOWLEDGE TREE (GET /profiles/me/tree): self-referenced DAG of concept nodes colored by node_state (locked/available/learning/mastered/needs_review). 'mastered' decays to 'needs_review' when now>decay_due_at (FSRS), making spaced review feel like tending a garden. Weekly personal recap optional ('you mastered 6 nodes, science branch 60% green'). NEVER rank-vs-others.

DAILY GOAL PRESETS (autonomy, changeable anytime): casual 5min/~50XP, regular 10min/~100XP, serious 15min/~150XP, intense 20min/~200XP.

ANTI-PATTERNS DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED/GATED: public leaderboards OFF by default (admin-gated, opt-in flag competitive_opt_in, ability-banded leagues of 15-30, personal-best framing only); no loss-framed/FOMO notifications; no pay/grind-to-win currency; no big-XP-loss penalties; informational (competence-signaling) not controlling reward framing to avoid overjustification (stronger in children). Optional opt-in challenge modes (timed_sprint, boss_review, no_hint, explain_it) never gate core progression.

DATA FIELDS used: profiles.total_xp; xp_events ledger; streak_state (current/longest/freeze_inventory/freezes_used/rest_days/is_perfect/repair_window); badge_definitions+profile_badges; skill_mastery (mastery/node_state/recent_accuracy/decay_due_at); profile_settings.daily_goal.
security_model:
LAYERED SANITIZE-CLASSIFY-VALIDATE (OWASP LLM01:2025; child-facing). Raw prompt is untrusted data END-TO-END; the generator NEVER receives it -- only the validated frozen Pydantic StructuredIntent. One-way flow: raw -> preprocess -> classifier -> StructuredIntent -> validate/route -> generator(validated fields only). Unit-tested: no raw_prompt parameter ever reaches generator functions.

LAYER 0 (rate limit, ratelimit.py middleware): reject >2000 chars / 8KB UTF-8; per-(profile_id, ip_hash) token bucket ~20/min,~300/day; HTTP 429; track injection_strikes. Keys on ip_hash/device hash because resume codes are anonymous and trivial to rotate.

LAYER 1 (deterministic preprocess.py, pure, cannot be injected): unicodedata.normalize('NFKC') then strip+flag U+200B/200C/200D/2060/FEFF, bidi U+202A-202E/2066-2069, all C0/C1 controls except \n \t; collapse whitespace; flag Latin+Cyrillic/Greek homoglyph mixing. Cheap regexes from patterns.yaml ('ignore all previous instructions','disregard','you are now','system prompt','DAN', leading Assistant/User/System role markers, long base64 blobs, payload-split markers) RAISE suspicion_score only, never block alone.

LAYER 2 (classifier.py, Haiku 4.5): per-request nonce delimiter (secrets.token_hex(8)) wrapping sanitized text in a tagged 'student_input' block declared untrusted; system prompt = untrusted_content_policy; Structured Outputs json_schema strict; static system prompt cached. Classifies, never answers.

LAYER 3 (validate.py, deterministic -- the real breakout containment): re-check all enums, re-sanitize topic, build the Decision tagged union. Routing: is_educational=false OR off_task=true -> Refuse; safety_flags contains self_harm -> Crisis (localized resources, NO LLM counseling); other safety_flags -> Refuse+redirect; classifier_confidence<0.5 -> Clarify; injection_detected=true -> log+strike, proceed with CLEAN fields only. NEVER rely on the prompt/delimiter alone (adaptive attackers defeat model-only defenses).

LAYER 4 (safety.py, child safety + crisis): categories self-harm, sexual, violence/weapons, illegal/dangerous, hate/harassment, PII solicitation, academic-integrity. self_harm -> non-dismissable Vue crisis card from crisis_resources.yaml (ISO country + global fallback: US 988, EU 112) + 'I am an AI, not a professional' disclosure (CA SB 243 / SAFE BOTs Act). Others refuse+redirect. Take-a-break nudges. Use grade_band+age+intent to AVOID over-blocking legitimate older-student topics (WWII, reproduction, chemistry).

LAYER 5 (audit.py, async fire-and-forget): record request_id, hashed_profile_id, ip_hash, ts, raw_length, language, removed_char_summary, suspicion_score, heuristic_hit_ids, classifier verdict (topic HASHED), decision_type, reason, latency_ms, model+PROMPT_VERSION, token_usage. DEFAULT NO RAW TEXT (COPPA 2026). RAW_CAPTURE_ON_FLAG (env, default false) retains Fernet-encrypted raw text for flagged events only, purged after audit_raw_retention_days.

VALIDATION RULES: StructuredIntent is frozen Pydantic v2, additionalProperties:false, all enums allowlisted (intent=study|test|refuse|crisis|clarify; subject allowlist+other; grade_band enum; language ISO-639-1; safety_flags enum array). Structured-output conformance is NOT the only check -- re-validate server-side.

OUTPUT SANITIZATION (frontend lib/safeContent.ts, defense the client owns): all LLM/Replicate output untrusted. Markdown via markdown-it{html:false} -> DOMPurify -> v-html only after sanitize. SVG via DOMPurify {USE_PROFILES:{svg:true,svgFilters:true}, FORBID_TAGS:['script','foreignObject'], FORBID_ATTR:[/^on/i]} (also server-side lxml strip on store: reject script/foreignObject/external href/on*, require viewBox, cap ~60KB). Math via KaTeX with conservative trust/maxExpand. NEVER raw v-html/:href/:src (javascript: not blocked). FastAPI sets strict CSP (no inline script; img-src self + Replicate CDN). Replicate safety_tolerance=1, safety checker ENABLED, plus Claude pre-moderation.

ADMIN: separate realm, JWT (HS256, 1h) + Argon2id; routes behind beforeEach auth guard; API keys stored Fernet-encrypted in app_settings, masked in responses, env overrides at boot. Resume code is server-issued opaque; client storage is a cache only, never trusted for authorization.
frontend_structure: STACK (pinned): vue@^3.5.31, vue-router@^4.5, pinia@^3 (flat setup stores), pinia-plugin-persistedstate@^4, @vueuse/core@^11, @vueuse/motion@^2, gsap@^3.13 (free incl. all plugins), @lottiefiles/dotlottie-vue, markdown-it@^14, markdown-it-katex, katex@^0.16, dompurify@^3, vuedraggable@^4 (vue.draggable.next), vue-i18n@^10/11, @fontsource/lexend, @fontsource/atkinson-hyperlegible, self-hosted OpenDyslexic woff2. devDeps: vite@^7, @vitejs/plugin-vue@^5, @tailwindcss/vite@^4, tailwindcss@^4 (CSS-first @theme, NO tailwind.config.js), typescript@~5.6, vue-tsc@^2, vitest@^2, @vue/test-utils@^2, @playwright/test, @axe-core/playwright, eslint@^9, prettier, openapi-typescript (generates src/types/api.ts from FastAPI OpenAPI -- the anti-drift seam). vite.config.ts plugins:[vue(), tailwindcss()]. ROUTES: '/' (Home = single dominant prompt hero), '/loading/:sessionId' (SSE progress), '/lesson/:sessionId' (LessonReader), '/test/:sessionId' (TestRunner), '/results/:sessionId' (Results+gamification), '/resume' (enter resume code), '/admin/*' (lazy + beforeEach auth guard, separate token). PINIA STORES (setup syntax): useSessionStore (resume code, profile, locale, XP/streak/badges), usePromptStore (raw prompt, sanitized intent, mode=lesson|test), useGenerationStore (SSE status queued|generating|ready|error, partial), useLessonStore (sections, media), useTestStore (questions[], current index, answers, scoring, submitAnswer), usePrefsStore (theme/font/fontScale/reducedMotion/sound/locale/dailyGoal -- persisted to localStorage + synced to server). The loading route opens EventSource, on 'ready' router.push to /lesson or /test by usePromptStore.mode. FOLDER: src/{ main.ts, App.vue, router/index.ts, stores/{session,prompt,generation,lesson,test,prefs}.ts, views/{HomeView,LoadingView,LessonView,TestView,ResultsView,ResumeView,admin/*}.vue, components/{common,layout,gamification}/*.vue, components/questions/{QuestionRenderer,McqQuestion,TrueFalseQuestion,FillBlankQuestion,MatchQuestion,OrderQuestion,HotspotQuestion,AnswerFeedback}.vue, components/content/{SafeContent,LessonSection,MathBlock}.vue, composables/{useReducedMotion,useCelebration,useGeneration,useApi}.ts, lib/{safeContent.ts,api.ts}, types/{question.ts,session.ts,api.ts}, locales/*.json }. QUESTION FAMILY (discriminated union by kind, exhaustive TS): type Question = Mcq|TrueFalse|FillBlank|Match|Order|Hotspot, each {id,kind,prompt,explanation?,...}. switches to the component. Each emits normalized AnswerEvent {questionId,value,isCorrect,latencyMs} -> useTestStore.submitAnswer -> POST /answers. Shared shows icon+color+localized text+optional sound+reduced-motion-aware micro-animation (NEVER color alone). Drag-drop (match/order) uses vuedraggable for pointer/touch BUT ships a mandatory select-then-place keyboard/click fallback (WCAG 2.1.1); hotspot uses real
visual_pipeline:
THREE-TIER HYBRID, all content-hash cached on disk (backend/app/visuals/*).

DECISION LOGIC (driven by visual_kind tag the lesson/visual-spec step emits per requested visual): visual_kind in {diagram, chart, labeled_figure, cycle, timeline, geometry, number_line, food_chain, map} -> TIER 1 CLAUDE-SVG (default, free, instant, ~70-80% of visuals). visual_kind in {illustration, scene, character, photo, texture} -> TIER 2 REPLICATE RASTER. visual_kind=icon/badge/decorative_vector -> recraft-ai/recraft-v3-svg. animation: diagrammatic motion -> SVG SMIL/CSS @keyframes or Lottie (default); cinematic 'bring this to life' (opt-in, quota-gated) -> TIER 3 Replicate image-to-video.

TIER 1 CLAUDE-SVG (default): a dedicated/embedded Claude Opus 4.8 call returns strict JSON {svg, alt, caption}. Server sanitize with lxml: reject <script>/<foreignObject>/external href/on* attrs, require viewBox, cap ~60KB; store sanitized SVG inline in visual_assets.svg_inline AND on disk. Real <text>/<title>/<desc>/ARIA labels = accessible + translatable (no diffusion text-mangling); recolor via currentColor/CSS vars for themes. Chosen because diffusion still misspells text (esp. non-English) and SVG costs $0.

TIER 2 REPLICATE RASTER (only for believable scenes/characters): config-driven model tier by layout_slot --
  RASTER_MODEL_DEFAULT = black-forest-labs/flux-2-dev (~$0.012/MP, ~2.5s) for HERO/INLINE_FIGURE/FULL_BLEED_MOBILE.
  RASTER_MODEL_CHEAP = black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-4b (Apache-2.0, sub-second) for QUIZ_THUMB/icon high-volume small assets.
  RASTER_MODEL_HERO = black-forest-labs/flux-2-pro ($0.015 + $0.015/MP, ~6s) for PORTRAIT_CHARACTER/mascot.
  flux-2-flex ($0.06/MP) only if in-image typography unavoidable. Legacy fallback flux-schnell ($0.003/img).
  Params: megapixels=1 (0.25-0.5 for thumbs), output_format=webp, safety_tolerance=1 (strictest, checker ENABLED), seed stored for reproducibility, up to 8 reference images for character/mascot consistency.

LAYOUT SLOTS -> aspect/MP/tier (LayoutSlot enum, derives Replicate params + Tailwind aspect-* class to reserve space/avoid CLS): HERO 16:9@1MP/dev; INLINE_FIGURE 4:3 or 1:1@1MP/dev; QUIZ_THUMB & icon 1:1@0.25-0.5MP/klein; PORTRAIT_CHARACTER 3:4@1MP/pro; FULL_BLEED_MOBILE 9:16@1MP/dev.

PROMPTING (NEVER raw student text -> Replicate): Claude rewrites intent into a fixed English template: 'A friendly, colorful flat vector illustration of {subject}, simple clean shapes, soft rounded edges, bright cheerful palette, plain off-white background, no text, no labels, educational childrens textbook style, age-appropriate for {grade}, safe for kids, clear and uncluttered, centered composition' + avoid clause 'Do not include: scary imagery, violence, blood, weapons, realistic human faces, brand logos, watermarks, or any written text.' MULTILINGUAL: generate LABEL-FREE language-neutral raster ONCE, overlay translated labels as SVG <text> per detected_lang (label_overlay_json in assets_refs) -> one cached image serves all languages, correct spelling guaranteed.

TIER 3 VIDEO (rare, opt-in, quota-gated): wan-video/wan-2.2-i2v-fast (~$0.018-0.05/s) or wan-2.5-i2v; kwaivgi/* (Kling) premium. Default to SVG/CSS/Lottie for almost all motion (deterministic, pedagogically correct, cacheable). Always @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) + pause control.

CACHE: key = SHA-256(canonical JSON {asset_type, model_id, model_version, normalized_prompt(NFC+lowercased+whitespace-collapsed), sorted params, seed, output_format}). Path data/cache/visuals/<h0:2>/<h2:4>/<hash>.<ext>; visual_assets row. Generate in FastAPI background task (cold starts), show skeleton, persist bytes immediately (Replicate URLs expire ~1h -- NEVER hotlink). Write atomically (temp+os.replace); INSERT OR IGNORE on hash PK (SQLite single-writer). Serve /assets/{hash} with Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable. LRU eviction by last_accessed_at+low hit_count. Docker named volume for data/cache survives redeploys. model_id+version in the key so an upgrade cleanly invalidates. Self-host ONLY flux-2-klein-4b weights (Apache-2.0); flux-2-dev is non-commercial weights -> use ONLY via Replicate hosted endpoint.
open_decisions: Resume-code UX & recovery: confirm 8-char Crockford base32 format and whether to also surface a QR/deeplink; decide the exact one-time-display + 're-show on demand' policy and any optional self-set passphrase to harden against shoulder-surfing without adding PII.

Real-time transport: SSE (recommended, simplest with FastAPI) vs WebSocket for the generation/loading stream and for live gamification toasts. Lock one before frontend and backend build the loading flow.

Background task runtime: plain FastAPI BackgroundTasks vs an in-process asyncio task queue vs adding a lightweight worker (e.g. arq/Dramatiq). SQLite single-writer + Docker Compose argues for in-process; confirm acceptable for expected concurrency and visual cold-starts.

Self-hosting vs hosted-only for Replicate: confirm staying 100% on Replicate hosted (pay-per-use, license-safe for flux-2-dev) vs self-hosting flux-2-klein-4b for cost at volume. Affects cost model and Docker footprint.

Exact mastery formula weights: the function mapping per-item FSRS retrievability+state to skill_mastery.mastery (state weights, aggregation, clamp) needs final numeric constants and a unit-test fixture before gamification and tree rendering are built against it.
Latency thresholds for FSRS Hard/Easy rating derivation: define per-item-type 'slow' and 'quick' latency_ms thresholds (static config vs adaptive percentile) since they directly drive scheduling.

Concept-graph bootstrapping: how concepts/edges/misconceptions are seeded vs grown lazily by the generator, and who curates the prereq edges that gate the knowledge tree (LLM-proposed + admin-approved?).

Crisis-resource coverage: finalize crisis_resources.yaml country list and legal review for jurisdictions served (CA SB 243 / SAFE BOTs scope) and the exact non-dismissable card copy + AI-disclosure wording.

i18n scope: which UI locales ship at launch vs auto-detected generation-only languages, and whether per-language native pedagogy exemplars (cached prefixes) are built for all or a top-N subset (cost vs quality).
Daily-cap & desired_retention defaults per age band, and whether desired_retention is user-visible or admin-only; affects review load and perceived difficulty.
Leaderboard policy: confirm OFF by default and whether opt-in ability-banded leagues ship at v1 at all given child-safety stance.
Batches pre-generation curation: which 'high-traffic' lesson/grade/language combinations to pre-generate nightly via the Batches API (50% off) and the eviction policy for the curated library cache.
risks: Prompt-injection / child-safety breakout: a jailbreak that reaches the generator is also a child-safety incident. Mitigated by the one-way flow (generator sees only validated fields) + deterministic validate layer, but model-only defenses fail against adaptive attackers; requires a maintained red-team injection corpus (tests/test_injection_corpus.py) in CI and ongoing pattern updates.

XSS via LLM/Replicate output: SVG (script/foreignObject/on*) and markdown are live XSS vectors against minors. Requires dual-layer sanitization (server lxml on store + client DOMPurify on render), strict CSP, and keeping DOMPurify/KaTeX patched (past SVG namespace-confusion bypasses).

Cost blow-up: uncapped Opus 4.8 generation + Replicate per-MP billing + video can escalate fast. Mitigated by prompt caching (must verify cache_read>0 or cost silently doubles), content-hash asset cache, megapixels pinned to 1, Batches for bulk, per-profile video quota, count_tokens guardrail; needs live cost dashboards + budget alerts.

Prompt-cache silent misses: any non-byte-identical pedagogy prefix (datetime, uuid, unsorted json) or a prefix under the 4096-token min on Opus 4.8 yields cache_creation with no error and full-price reads; must be asserted in tests and monitored via generation_usage.

FSRS/mastery integration drift: gamification, tree, and badges all key off the derived mastery formula; if its constants change without a versioned fixture, modules diverge. Lock the formula + unit fixtures before parallel build.

SQLite single-writer contention: concurrent generation + answer grading + asset cache inserts can hit write locks. Mitigated by INSERT OR IGNORE, atomic file writes, WAL mode, funneling writes; but SQLite caps real concurrency -- revisit if load grows.

Readability mis-calibration for non-English: FKGL is English-only; relying on it for other languages mislabels difficulty. Mitigated by proxy metrics + readability_note, but proxies are weaker; risk of age-inappropriate text in less-resourced languages.

Desirable-difficulty perception gap: spacing/interleaving feel harder and lower short-term scores; learners (and parents) may rate the tool 'less effective' without UI framing of long-term retention gains and streaks.

Streak/gamification harm to vulnerable young users: streaks and competition can create anxiety/compulsion; mitigated by bounded forgiveness, rest days, no FOMO, leaderboards off, age-band kill-switches -- but needs ongoing review and possibly parental/age gating.

Replicate dependency & churn: model IDs/prices iterate fast (FLUX.2 launched Nov 2025); URL expiry (~1h) and cold starts. Mitigated by config-driven model_id+version in the cache key and immediate byte persistence; needs periodic re-verification of IDs/pricing.

Crisis-routing correctness & legal exposure: mis-routing a self-harm case (LLM counseling instead of resources) is both a safety failure and a regulatory one (CA SB 243 / SAFE BOTs). Requires legal review, localized resources, and conservative classification (false-positive bias acceptable).

Opus 4.8 behavioral shifts: 4.8 narrates more / asks more / can leak reasoning if thinking disabled. Mitigated by adaptive thinking ON, length/format exemplars in cached prefix, full-spec-in-one-turn; quiz explanation verbosity must be capped with a positive exemplar or it bloats cost and UI.
